entries.title: Severity: High |  Title: Poly Clariti Manager - Multiple Security Vulnerabilities
entries.description:
Description: Multiple potential security vulnerabilities have been identified on Poly Clariti Manager, on-premise management platform. HP has addressed the issues in the latest software update. 
  CVEs: 
  HP ID: HBSBPY04037 Rev. 1
  Published: Tue, 22 Jul 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: Medium |  Title: Certain HP DesignJet products  Information disclosure
entries.description:
Description: Certain HP DesignJet products may be vulnerable to information disclosure though printer's web interface allowing unauthenticated users to view sensitive print job information.
  CVEs: CVE-2025-3508
  HP ID: HPSBPI04039 Rev. 1
  Published: Fri, 25 Jul 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: Medium |  Title: HP Linux Imaging and Printing Software - Use of DSA Key
entries.description:
Description: A potential security vulnerability has been identified in the HP Linux Imaging and Printing Software documentation. This potential vulnerability is due to the use of a weak code signing key, Digital Signature Algorithm (DSA). 
  CVEs: CVE-2025-43023
  HP ID: HPSBPI04033 Rev. 1
  Published: Fri, 25 Jul 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: High |  Title: Intel PROSet/Wireless WiFi February 2025 Security Update
entries.description:
Description: Intel has informed HP of potential security vulnerabilities in some Intel PROSet/Wireless WiFi and Killer WiFi software products, which might allow information disclosure or denial of service. Intel is releasing firmware and software updates to mitigate these potential vulnerabilities.
  CVEs: CVE-2024-41168 CVE-2024-39356 CVE-2024-39606 CVE-2024-40887 CVE-2024-41166 CVE-2024-36285 CVE-2024-39271
  HP ID: HPSBHF04012 Rev. 3
  Published: Mon, 28 Jul 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: Medium |  Title: Certain HP LaserJet Pro Printers  Potential Information Disclosure
entries.description:
Description: Certain HP LaserJet Pro printers may be vulnerable to information disclosure when a non-authenticated user queries a devices local address book.
  CVEs: CVE-2025-43018
  HP ID: HPSBPI04040 Rev. 1
  Published: Wed, 30 Jul 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: High |  Title: HP UEFI Firmware February 2022 Security Updates
entries.description:
Description: Potential vulnerabilities have been identified in UEFI firmware (BIOS) for some PC products which may allow escalation of privilege and arbitrary code execution. HP is releasing firmware updates to mitigate these potential vulnerabilities.
  CVEs: CVE-2021-39298 CVE-2021-39297 CVE-2021-39299 CVE-2021-39300 CVE-2021-39301
  HP ID: HPSBHF03765 Rev. 9
  Published: Thu, 31 Jul 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: Medium |  Title: Elan Fingerprint Sensor Security Update
entries.description:
Description: A potential vulnerability has been identified in certain HP PC products using Elan fingerprint sensor devices, which might allow operating system authentication bypass by spoofing. HP is releasing BIOS and fingerprint sensor driver updates to address the potential vulnerability. 
  CVEs: CVE-2024-0454
  HP ID: HPSBHF04015 Rev. 2
  Published: Mon, 04 Aug 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: Medium |  Title: AMD Graphics Driver February 2025 Security Update
entries.description:
Description: AMD has informed HP of potential vulnerabilities identified in some AMD Graphics Drivers, which might allow denial of service, loss of confidentiality, loss of integrity, or loss of availability. AMD is releasing AMD Graphics Driver for Windows to mitigate these vulnerabilities.
  CVEs: CVE-2024-21971 CVE-2023-20508
  HP ID: HPSBHF04002 Rev. 2
  Published: Tue, 05 Aug 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: High |  Title: Intel PROSet/Wireless WiFi Software May 2025 Security Update
entries.description:
Description: Intel has informed HP of potential security vulnerabilities in some Intel PROSet/Wireless WiFi Software products, which might allow denial of service. Intel is releasing software updates to mitigate these potential vulnerabilities.
  CVEs: CVE-2025-20006 CVE-2025-20032 CVE-2025-20618 CVE-2025-20046 CVE-2025-20026 CVE-2025-20062 CVE-2025-20039
  HP ID: HPSBHF04019 Rev. 2
  Published: Wed, 06 Aug 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: High |  Title: AMD Client Processors February 2025 Security Update
entries.description:
Description: AMD has informed HP of potential vulnerabilities in platform components for some AMD Client Processors, which might allow arbitrary code execution, loss of integrity, loss of confidentiality, or loss of availability. AMD is releasing firmware updates to mitigate these vulnerabilities.
  CVEs: CVE-2023-31342 CVE-2023-31343 CVE-2023-31345 CVE-2023-31331 CVE-2023-20507
  HP ID: HPSBHF04004 Rev. 2

entries.title: Severity: High |  Title: Intel Graphics Software February 2025 Security Update
entries.description:
Description: Intel has informed HP of potential security vulnerabilities in some Intel Graphics software, which might allow escalation of privilege or denial of service. Intel is releasing software updates to mitigate these potential vulnerabilities
  CVEs: CVE-2024-37355 CVE-2024-42410 CVE-2024-38310
  HP ID: HPSBHF04006 Rev. 2
  Published: Mon, 11 Aug 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: High |  Title: Intel PROSet/Wireless WiFi Software August 2025 Security Update
entries.description:
Description: Intel has informed HP of a potential security vulnerability for some Intel PROSet/Wireless WiFi Software for Windows, which might allow denial of service. Intel is releasing software updates to mitigate this potential vulnerability.
  CVEs: CVE-2025-20625
  HP ID: HPSBHF04041 Rev. 1
  Published: Tue, 12 Aug 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.description:
Description: AMD has informed HP of potential vulnerabilities identified in some AMD client processor platforms, which might lead to loss of confidentiality, loss of integrity, arbitrary code execution, loss of availability, or denial of service. AMD is releasing firmware updates to mitigate these vulnerabilities.
  CVEs: CVE-2024-36326 CVE-2024-36354 CVE-2024-21947 CVE-2023-31325 CVE-2025-0032 CVE-2021-26383 CVE-2024-21970 CVE-2021-26377 CVE-2021-46757 CVE-2023-20572 CVE-2024-21977 CVE-2023-313

entries.title: Severity: High |  Title: HP Security Manager  Potential Remote Code Execution
entries.description:
Description: HP Security Manager is potentially vulnerable to Remote Code Execution due to the use of Microsoft OLE Database library version 18.4 for Microsofts SQL Server Express and Microsofts SQL Server.
  CVEs: CVE-2024-29044 CVE-2024-28906
  HP ID: HPSBPI04049 Rev. 1
  Published: Thu, 21 Aug 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: High |  Title: Poly Video and Voice Devices - Potential Arbitrary Code Execution
entries.description:
Description: A vulnerability in the FreeType font library affects Poly Video and Voice devices that are on the Android platform. This might result in arbitrary code execution. 
  CVEs: CVE-2025-27363
  HP ID: HPSBPY04038 Rev. 1
  Published: Fri, 29 Aug 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: Medium |  Title: Certain Poly Devices  Path Traversal Vulnerability - Arbitrary File Access by Unauthorized User
entries.description:
Description: A vulnerability was discovered in the firmware builds up to 8.2.1.0820 in certain Poly devices. The firmware flaw does not properly prevent path traversal and could lead to information disclosure.
  CVEs: CVE-2025-0858
  HP ID: HPSBPY03996 Rev. 4
  Published: Fri, 29 Aug 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: High |  Title: Poly Lens Desktop Application  Privilege Escalation
entries.description:
Description: A vulnerability in the Poly Lens Desktop application running on the Windows platform might allow modifications to the filesystem, which might lead to SYSTEM level privileges being granted.
  CVEs: CVE-2025-43491
  HP ID: HPSBPY04048 Rev. 1
  Published: Tue, 09 Sep 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: Critical |  Title: Certain HP LaserJet Pro, HP LaserJet Enterprise, HP LaserJet Managed Printers - Potential Remote Code Execution and Potential Elevation of Privilege
entries.description:
Description: Certain HP LaserJet Pro, HP LaserJet Enterprise, and HP LaserJet Managed Printers may potentially be vulnerable to Remote Code Execution and Elevation of Privilege when processing a PostScript print job.
  CVEs: CVE-2025-26506 CVE-2025-26508 CVE-2025-26507
  HP ID: HPSBPI04007 Rev. 4
  Published: Tue, 09 Sep 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: High |  Title: AMD Client UEFI Firmware  SMM Lock Bypass
entries.description:

  CVEs: CVE-2023-31315
  HP ID: HPSBHF03993 Rev. 4
  Published: Wed, 10 Sep 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.description:

  HP ID: HPSBHF04016 Rev. 3
  Published: Fri, 12 Sep 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.description:
Description: Intel has informed HP of potential security vulnerabilities in firmware for some Intel Processors, which might allow escalation of privilege, denial of service, or information disclosure. Intel is releasing firmware updates to mitigate these potential vulnerabilities.
  CVEs: CVE-2023-34440 CVE-2023-43758 CVE-2024-24582 CVE-2024-28127 CVE-2024-29214 CVE-2024-31155 CVE-2024-21859 CVE-2024-28047 CVE-2024-31157 CVE-2024-36293 CVE-2024-39279

entries.title: Severity: Medium |  Title: HyperX NGENUITY - Arbitrary Code Execution
entries.description:
Description: HyperX NGENUITY software is potentially vulnerable to arbitrary code execution. HP is releasing updated software to address the potential vulnerability.
  CVEs: CVE-2025-10568
  HP ID: HPSBHF04050 Rev. 1
  Published: Thu, 18 Sep 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.description:

Intel is releasing updates to mitigate these potential vulnerabilities.
  CVEs: CVE-2024-38307 CVE-2024-30211 CVE-2024-26021
  HP ID: HPSBHF04011 Rev. 3

  CVEs: CVE-2024-40431 CVE-2022-25478 CVE-2022-25480 CVE-2024-40432 CVE-2022-25477 CVE-2022-25479
  HP ID: HPSBHF04056 Rev. 1
  Published: Thu, 25 Sep 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.description:

  CVEs: CVE-2023-45230 CVE-2023-45234 CVE-2023-45235 CVE-2023-45232 CVE-2023-45233 CVE-2023-45229 CVE-2023-45231 CVE-2023-45236 CVE-2023-45237
  HP ID: HPSBHF03983 Rev. 3

entries.title: Severity: Medium |  Title: HP Support Assistant - Potential Escalation of Privilege
entries.description:
Description: A potential security vulnerability has been identified in the HP Support Assistant for versions prior to 9.47.41.0. The vulnerability could potentially allow a local attacker to escalate privileges via an arbitrary file write.
  CVEs: CVE-2025-10578
  HP ID: HPSBGN04053 Rev. 1
  Published: Tue, 30 Sep 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: High |  Title: HP Sure Start IFD Protection - BIOS Security Update
entries.description:
Description: A potential security vulnerability has been identified in HP Sure Starts protection of the Intel Flash Descriptor in certain HP PC products, which might allow security bypass, arbitrary code execution, loss of integrity or confidentiality, or denial of service. HP is releasing BIOS updates to mitigate the potential vulnerability.
  CVEs: CVE-2023-6215
  HP ID: HPSBHF04061 Rev. 1
  Published: Tue, 07 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: Medium |  Title: Intel 2024.3 IPU  Chipset Firmware Security Update
entries.description:
Description: Intel has informed HP of potential security vulnerabilities in Intel Converged Security and Manageability Engine (CSME), Intel Active Management Technology (AMT), Intel Standard Manageability, Intel PROSet/Wireless and Intel Killer Wi-Fi software, which might allow escalation of privilege, denial of service, or information disclosure. Intel is releasing firmware updates to mitigate these potential vulnerabilities.
  CVEs: CVE-2023-38655 CVE-2023-40067 CVE-2023-34424 CVE-2023-35061 C

  CVEs: CVE-2025-20074
  HP ID: HPSBHF04042 Rev. 2

entries.description:
Description: AMD has informed HP of potential vulnerabilities identified in some AMD processors, which might allow loss of confidentiality. AMD is providing Platform Initialization (PI) firmware updates to mitigate this vulnerability.
  CVEs: CVE-2024-36350 CVE-2024-36357
  HP ID: HPSBHF04035 Rev. 3
  Published: Mon, 20 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: High |  Title: Insyde BIOS Buffer Overflow in certain HP ARM-Based PCs
entries.description:

  CVEs: CVE-2022-35407

  CVEs: CVE-2024-0150 CVE-2024-0147 CVE-2024-0131
  HP ID: HPSBHF04014 Rev. 3
  Published: Thu, 30 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

entries.title: Severity: Medium |  Title: HP Card Readers (B Models)  Potential Information Disclosure
entries.description:
Description: The following HP Card Readers B Models (X3D03B & Y7C05B) are potentially vulnerable to information disclosure, allowing prior user identity to be inherited under certain conditions e.g., when an NFC device (such as a smartphone/smartwatches) is in proximity during a card swipe event.
  CVEs: CVE-2025-11998
  HP ID: HPSBPI04065 Rev. 2
  Published: Thu, 30 Oct 2025 00:00:00 GMT

  CVEs: CVE-2025-43490

entries.title: HPE unifies global distribution with Ingram Micro and TD SYNNEX
entries.description: HOUSTON – May 14, 2026 - HPE (NYSE: HPE) today named Ingram Micro and TD SYNNEX as its two global distribution partners, accelerating HPE’s move to a unified distribution model that delivers greater simplicity and consistency for partners. The unified model is expected to drive additional investment in dedicated HPE enablement resources, strengthen operational support, and help partners sell, deliver, and service solutions across HPE’s portfolio. The scale and expertise of two global distributio

entries.title: HPE Discover Celebration: Where partnership finds its rhythm
entries.description: May 19, 2026 – Every great band has a rhythm – trust built over time, distinct strengths working together, and the confidence to deliver when the lights come on. That’s HPE Discover. Customers, partners, and HPE teams coming together to turn bold ideas into real outcomes and prove what’s possible when partnership performs at its best. Celebration is where we feel it. It’s our moment to step out of the sessions, turn up the volume, and recognize the momentum we’ve built together. Read on to see h

entries.title: Liverpool John Moores University invests in student hardship fund through HPE’s Circular IT Program
entries.description: Liverpool John Moores University (LJMU) is tackling electronic waste by giving its retired IT equipment a second life with HPE Financial Services (HPEFS) and DTP Group in a way that is secure, sustainable and socially beneficial. The university wanted to stop equipment from ending up in landfill, maximize the value of reusable assets, and reinvest proceeds into technology upgrades and student welfare. The university has implemented HPE’s IT Asset Disposition (ITAD) services to adopt a circular e

entries.title: HPE positioned highest in execution and furthest in vision in 2026 Magic Quadrant™ for Enterprise Wired and Wireless LAN Infrastructure by Gartner® for fifth consecutive time
entries.description: HOUSTON, Texas – May 20, 2026 – HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced Gartner has recognized HPE as a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Enterprise Wired and Wireless LAN Infrastructure, positioning the company furthest in "Completeness of Vision" and highest for "Ability to Execute." This marks the fifth consecutive time that Gartner has placed HPE in this position, and the 20th time in a row that HPE has been in the Leaders Quadrant of this report. Completeness of Vision is evaluated on c

entries.title: Engineering innovation at scale: reflections from HPE Tech Con
entries.description: HPE Tech Con is not a conventional conference. It is a place where team members who have been chosen via a rigorous proposal and review process come together to examine hard problems, challenge assumptions, and nurture ideas. An innovation engine for the company, it shortens feedback loops between research, product engineering and customers, exposes early-stage technologies to real-world constraints, and enables systems-level thinking to take precedence over component-level optimization. Custome

entries.title: FASTFIVE selects HPE Aruba Networking SSE as its digital backbone to strengthen security and optimize IT operations
entries.description: Seoul, May 27, 2026 – HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced that FASTFIVE, a leading coworking space provider in South Korea, has selected HPE Aruba Networking SSE to strengthen secure user access to applications and streamline IT operations across its nationwide footprint of more than 60 office locations. With this SSE implementation, FASTFIVE gains secure hybrid access, centralized policy management, and a scalable foundation towards SASE that supports the company’s rapid business expansion. FASTFIV

entries.title: HPE to present live webcast of Investor Relations Summit at HPE Discover 2026
entries.description: HOUSTON – May 21, 2026 – HPE (NYSE: HPE) will conduct a live audio webcast of the Investor Relations Summit at HPE Discover 2026 on June 16, 2026. HPE also invites investors to view additional live programming from the event, including HPE president and CEO Antonio Neri’s keynote address. HPE Discover 2026 programming for investors will start at 11 a.m. CT (12 p.m. ET) on June 16 with Neri’s keynote address. The keynote will be broadcast live, as will the HPE Networking General Session later tha

entries.title: The supercomputer that started it all: Honoring Cray-1 on the $1 American coin
entries.description: It’s not every day a supercomputer is honored on a coin. That’s why, as a global HPC community, we couldn’t help but rejoice when the U.S. Mint releases a $1 American coin commemorating the Cray-1 supercomputer.

entries.title: Modern connectivity for modern care: Mercy Health selects HPE to upgrade aged care across 40+ sites in Australia
entries.description: Melbourne, Australia – May 27, 2026 – HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced that Mercy Health, one of Australia’s leading not-for-profit health and aged care providers, has selected HPE to modernize connectivity across its Healthy Ageing and Residential Aged Care facilities nationwide. The initiative will deliver a secure, high-performance connectivity platform across more than 40 sites, supporting over 2,600 aged care residents and more than 400 retirement village units, as well as staff, families an

entries.title: HPE and Rowan University expand partnership to accelerate research and strengthen student workforce readiness
entries.description: HOUSTON and Glassboro, N.J. – May 28, 2026 – HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced an expanded partnership with Rowan University to accelerate research, strengthen student training, and support the university’s continued growth. Delivered in partnership with SHI International, one of the world’s leading technology solution providers, the initiative will enhance the performance and resilience of Rowan’s technology environment to enable faster insight, more hands-on learning, and a better-connected camp

entries.title: HPE introduces CPU server with NVIDIA-Vera CPU, purpose-built for Agentic AI
entries.description: HOUSTON and TAIPEI – COMPUTEX – June 1, 2026 – HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced the expansion of its industry-leading server portfolio with the introduction of the HPE ProLiant Compute DL394 Gen12, powered by NVIDIA Vera CPU. This next-generation server is engineered specifically to address the compute demands of emerging high-performance AI and data processing workloads delivering industry leading agentic AI CPU performance, memory bandwidth and low latency. Built on a foundation of advanced arc

entries.title: HPE names Chris Hsu to Board of Directors
entries.description: HOUSTON — June 1, 2026 — HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced the appointment of Christopher (Chris) Hsu, a Partner at Elliott Investment Management L.P., to its Board of Directors, effective immediately. Hsu will join the Board's Strategy Committee, as well as its Finance & Investment Committee. Hsu serves as Head of Portfolio Operations and Co-Lead of Private Equity at Elliott. Prior to joining the investment firm, he was CEO of Azibo, CEO of Micro Focus International and held leadership positions

entries.title: HPE reports fiscal 2026 second quarter results
entries.description: HOUSTON – June 1, 2026 – HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced financial results for the second quarter ended April 30, 2026. “HPE delivered an exceptional quarter with record-breaking revenue, higher-than-anticipated profitability, and increased free cash flow, reflecting strong execution and healthy demand across the business,” said Antonio Neri, president and CEO of HPE. “Customers continue to invest in modernizing their infrastructure and scaling AI, and our performance shows the strength of our c

entries.title: S k y Co., Ltd. accelerates secure AI development with HPE Private Cloud AI
entries.description: Tokyo, Japan – June 10, 2026 – HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced that S k y Co., Ltd. has deployed HPE Private Cloud AI, a turnkey AI factory solution that is part of the NVIDIA AI Computing by HPE portfolio. Delivered in just one month, the solution enables the company to safely harness sensitive data while accelerating AI-driven development and internal workflows under strict data governance. S k y Co., Ltd. is a leading Japanese technology company with expertise in software development and syst

entries.title: The Power of One: Helping partners unlock ambition at HPE Discover 2026
entries.description: At HPE Discover Las Vegas 2026, the message to partners is simple: the opportunity ahead is bigger when we move as one. Through the Power of One, HPE is helping partners unlock ambition with one portfolio, one program, and one experience. That means bringing together innovation that keeps partners relevant, expertise that helps them differentiate, and margin opportunities that support profitable growth. It also means reducing complexity so partners can spend less time navigating systems and more

entries.title: HPE fuels partner growth with new incentives, partner-led offers, and unified program
entries.description: LAS VEGAS – HPE Discover Las Vegas 2026 – June 15, 2026 – HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced it is unifying the HPE and Juniper Networks partner programs under HPE Partner Ready Vantage, creating a single, global program for partners to build, sell, and offer services across networking, cloud, and AI. Beginning Nov. 1, 2026, partners will operate under one program with a simplified structure, aligned incentives, and a consistent engagement model, while existing investments are protected. The unifie

entries.title: Honoring the HPE Partner of the Year 2026 Award winners for turning partnership into customer success
entries.description: HPE is proud to recognize the HPE Partner of the Year 2026 Award winners, honored at the HPE Partner Growth Summit and HPE Discover in Las Vegas. These prestigious awards recognize more than business momentum. They celebrate the outstanding work partners are doing every day to help customers navigate change, solve complex challenges, and achieve their growth ambitions. The HPE Partner of the Year 2026 Awards recognize HPE partners who exemplify the strength of our collaboration and demonstrate a

entries.title: HPE advances quantum computing at scale with expanded industry collaborations
entries.description: LAS VEGAS – HPE Discover Las Vegas 2026 - June 15, 2026 – HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced it has expanded relationships with eight companies to integrate high performance computing (HPC) and quantum computing systems to pave the way for practical and scalable hybrid classical-quantum applications in the future.

entries.title: Fighting fraud intelligently: HPE Nonstop Compute deploys agentic AI software for transaction processing with Lusis TANGO AIF
entries.description: In every type of real-time transaction environment, fraud is getting more sophisticated, faster, and harder to spot. Whether it’s bot-driven testing, account takeovers, synthetic identities or coordinated fraud rings, bad actors are continuously evolving. With each new tactic, it becomes more difficult for traditional threshold-based engines and manual interventions to respond. When teams can’t act fast enough, fraud gets through. When they over-correct, false positives increase, approvals slow,

entries.title: HPE brings agentic AI into production with NVIDIA, delivering security, governance, scale, and sovereignty
entries.description: LAS VEGAS – HPE Discover Las Vegas 2026 - June 16, 2026 – HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced new innovations to help customers transform into agentic enterprises and move AI into production with greater security, governance, and control. These new offerings transform the HPE AI Factory with NVIDIA for the next era of AI where intelligence adapts, evolves, collaborates, and governs. “As AI becomes more autonomous, organizations need a new architecture to run it securely, govern it responsibly, and s

entries.title: HPE expands self-driving networks across edge, campus, data center, and AI factories
entries.description: LAS VEGAS – HPE Discover Las Vegas 2026 – June 16, 2026 – HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced major advancements that expand its self-driving networking strategy across AI factories, data centers, and the enterprise edge by introducing new AI data center networking, routing, Agentic AIOps, and security innovations designed to simplify operations and improve performance across increasingly distributed AI-driven environments. Innovations introduced today advance networking as the foundation of HPE’s a

entries.title: Siemens Energy chooses HPE to transform engineering with AI as global power demand surges
entries.description: LAS VEGAS – HPE Discover Las Vegas 2026 - June 16, 2026 – HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced that Siemens Energy, a global leader in energy technology, is deploying a dedicated global platform through GreenLake for engineering-driven high-performance computing as the company responds to record global demand for energy. Powered by NVIDIA AI Computing by HPE – the portfolio of AI factory solutions co-developed by HPE and NVIDIA – the platform for the first time introduces accelerated computing at sca

entries.title: Vultr selects HPE and NVIDIA for next-generation AI infrastructure for cloud-scale data centers
entries.description: LAS VEGAS – HPE Discover Las Vegas 2026 – June 17, 2026 – HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced Vultr, the world’s largest privately held hyperscaler, has selected HPE and NVIDIA for large-scale AI datacenter deployments designed to support rapidly growing enterprise demand for private cloud and AI workloads. Vultr has selected the NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 by HPE connected with NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet networking, part of the NVIDIA AI Computing by HPE portfolio, to power its next-generation AI infrastruc

entries.title: With data gravity reshaping the enterprise, HPE and Lumen are building the dynamic architecture to match
entries.description: Enterprise IT architectures are undergoing a fundamental shift. For years, data moved to a single central location, where applications were run. But with AI, real-time analytics, and exploding data volumes at the edge and across the enterprise, the approach to data has flipped. Data has mass and attracts applications, services, and processes toward where it resides, whether that’s on premises, at the edge, or in a hyperscaler. At the same time, CPU, GPU, power, and cooling capacity create their

entries.title: HPE delivers unified agentic IT operations with GreenLake and HPE Morpheus Software
entries.description: LAS VEGAS – HPE Discover Las Vegas 2026 – June 17, 2026 – HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced expanded capabilities across GreenLake to deliver unified operations for the agentic enterprise through innovations across agentic AIOps, private cloud, and software for AI infrastructure. Through GreenLake, HPE provides customers with a unified operating model that addresses rising virtualization costs, modernizes for emerging AI workloads, and simplifies operational complexity. “As enterprises scale AI, t

entries.title: HPE simplifies the supercomputing experience for sovereign AI research and large enterprises
entries.description: June 22, 2026 –As high performance computing (HPC) converges with artificial intelligence (AI) and quantum computing, supercomputing customers are required to find the system performance, flexibility and reliability to fuel the research that will create world-changing discoveries. With its latest innovations, HPE delivers powerful, efficient system architecture to support customers’ multi-faceted workloads and rise to the great responsibilities of this converged era. entries.title: HPE delivers six out of ten of world’s most powerful supercomputers
entries.description: June 23, 2026 – HPE builds supercomputers that empower its customers to push the limits of what’s possible in high performance computing (HPC). On the June 2026 TOP500, HPE-built systems have claimed 6 spots out of the top 10 list of the world’s fastest supercomputers and three of those are among the only five that are verified exascale supercomputers, and those are El Capitan at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, Frontier at Oak Ridge National Laboratory, and Aurora at Argonne National Lab

entries.title: Honoring America’s innovation story and building what comes next
entries.description: Houston – July 3, 2026 – As the United States marks its 250th anniversary, its story of progress continues to be shaped by a distinctly American spirit of ingenuity: bold ideas, scientific discovery, entrepreneurial ambition, and the belief that progress is strongest when it creates opportunity for others.

entries.title: Using AI to build a more resilient network — from the inside out
entries.description: Vulnerabilities are a reality of every technological device and code base in existence. This has been true for as long as I’ve been in security. The difference is that with AI, the speed and scale at which they’re discovered and exploited is exponentially greater. AI is a double-edged sword in security. The same capability that lets defenders find vulnerabilities faster also puts that power in the hands of attackers. That's what makes Anthropic's Claude Mythos so significant — a model trained to

entries.title: HPE releases annual Living Progress Report as AI uptake makes efficient, secure, and responsible digital infrastructure even more critical
entries.description: HOUSTON, Texas, July 13, 2026 – HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced the publication of its 11th annual Living Progress Report, detailing the company’s Fiscal Year 2025 (FY25) progress against its Living Progress strategy. The report highlights how HPE enables customers to operate effectively as rising demand for compute-intensive workloads like AI place greater pressure on energy, resources, regulation, and trust.
